perturbation.original_text: 6. Term and Termination 6.1 If either Party decides not to become, or continue to be involved in the Purpose it shall notify the other Party in writing immediately. The obligations of each Party shall, notwithstanding any earlier termination of negotiations or di scussions between the Parties in relation to the Purpose, continue for a period of five (5) years from the termination of this A greement. 6.2 Termination of this Agreement shall not affect any accrued rights or remedies to whic h either Party is entitled.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. No Waiver 9.1 Failure by either Party to enforce any of its rights under this Agreement shall not be taken as or deemed to be a waiver of such. 9.2 No waiver or amendment of any provisions of this Agreement shall be valid or binding against either Party unless the waiver or amendment is made in writing and signed by the duly appointed representatives of both Parties . 6. Term and Termination 6.1 If either Party decides not to become, or continue to be involved in the Purpose it shall notify the other Party in writing immediately. The obligations of each Party shall, notwithstanding any earlier termination of negotiations or di scussions between the Parties in relation to the Purpose, continue for a period of five (5) years from the termination of this A greement. 6.2 Termination of this Agreement shall not affect any accrued rights or remedies to whic h either Party is entitled.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the 'Term and Termination' clause to the 'No Waiver' section will hide it away and make it less visible to both parties. Termination rights are usually stand alone to showcase that this can happen, but the relocation can bring cause for legal disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_law: There should be good faith and fair dealing during the course of the contract.
perturbation.law_explanation: The relocation of the 'Term and Termination' clause may violate the covenant of good faith and fair dealing, especially if one party uses the obscurity of the clause to unfairly terminate the agreement. This relocation can be seen as 'not good faith'. The parties will be caught off guard, and it would be hard to see the termination clause as this will cause issues with good faith and fair dealings.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 1-304. Obligation of Good Faith. | Uniform Commercial Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you § 1-304. Obligation of Good Faith. Every contract or duty within the Uniform Commercial Code imposes an obligation of good faith in its performance and enforcement. Uniform Commercial Code Toolbox About Uniform Laws State Uniform Commercial Codes
perturbation.justification: The structural flaw of relocating the 'Term and Termination' clause to the 'No Waiver' section creates a legal contradiction because it undermines the clarity and accessibility of a crucial contractual right. The Law Explanation suggests this relocation could violate the covenant of good faith and fair dealing, particularly if it leads to unfair termination due to obscurity. While Scraped Law Snippet 2 is missing, Scraped Law Snippet 1, referencing UCC § 1-304, emphasizes the 'obligation of good faith' in contract performance. By burying the termination clause, the changed text arguably contradicts this obligation, as it could be perceived as an attempt to obscure important terms, potentially leading to disputes and undermining fair dealing. The relocation makes it difficult for parties to locate and easily interpret the termination clause, supporting the claim that this structural flaw creates a legal contradiction.
